title: Shri Karwa Chauth Vrat Katha
intro: Read Shri Karwa Chauth Vrat Katha in English and pray for marital bliss, good fortune, and your husband’s long life.
description:
Karva Chauth Vrat Katha

Long ago, there lived a pious and learned Brahmin with his family.
He lived a peaceful and virtuous life.

He had seven sons and one beautiful daughter.
Her name was Veeravati.

Being the only sister, she was dearly loved by all her brothers.
They always cared for her happiness.

When the time came, Veeravati was married to a noble Brahmin youth.
A few months after her marriage, she came to her parents' home.

On that day, she observed the sacred Karva Chauth fast.
She kept the fast for the long life and well-being of her husband.

She remained without food or water throughout the day.
By evening, she became extremely weak and restless.

When her brothers returned home, they noticed her condition.
They asked the reason for her suffering.

Veeravati explained that she could eat only after offering water to the Moon.
She had to wait until the Moon appeared.

Her brothers could not bear to see her in pain.
The youngest brother came up with a plan.

He lit a lamp and placed it behind a sieve on a Peepal tree.
From a distance, it looked just like the Moon.

The brothers told Veeravati that the Moon had risen.
They asked her to complete the ritual and break her fast.

Trusting her brothers, Veeravati offered Arghya to the false Moon.
She then sat down to eat her meal.

As soon as she took her first bite, she sneezed.
She ignored it and continued eating.

In the second bite, she found a strand of hair.
Still, she paid no attention to the warning.

The moment she took the third bite, a tragic message arrived.
She was informed that her husband had passed away.

Veeravati was heartbroken and burst into tears.
She could not bear the terrible news.

Her sister-in-law then revealed the truth.
She explained that Veeravati had broken her fast before the real Moon had risen.

That mistake had caused the unfortunate result.
Veeravati deeply regretted her actions.

She resolved to bring her husband back through her devotion.
She did not allow his final rites to be performed.

For an entire year, she cared for his body with faith.
She prayed sincerely every single day.

When Karva Chauth came again the following year,
all her sisters-in-law visited her.

Veeravati humbly requested each of them.
"Take the needle of Yama and give me the needle of my beloved. Make me a fortunate wife again."

Finally, the youngest sister-in-law arrived.
She agreed to help after seeing Veeravati's unwavering devotion.

She gently cut her little finger.
She placed a few drops of divine nectar-like blood into the husband's mouth.

At that very moment, Veeravati's husband came back to life.
Their married life was restored with happiness and blessings.

Since then, married women observe the Karva Chauth fast with great devotion.
It is believed that listening to this sacred story brings marital bliss, good fortune, and a long life for one's husband.

faq.q1: What is the significance of Karva Chauth Vrat Katha?
faq.a1: Karva Chauth Vrat Katha symbolizes devotion, faith, and the sacred bond between husband and wife. Listening to the Katha is considered an important part of the Karva Chauth fast.
faq.q2: When should Karva Chauth Vrat Katha be recited?
faq.a2: It is traditionally recited on the day of Karva Chauth during the evening puja before offering Arghya to the Moon.
faq.q3: Who can listen to or recite the Karva Chauth Vrat Katha?
faq.a3: Anyone can read or listen to the Katha. Married women usually recite it while observing the Karva Chauth fast.
faq.q4: Why is Veeravati important in the Karva Chauth Katha?
faq.a4: Veeravati is the central character whose unwavering devotion and determination restored her husband’s life, highlighting the importance of faith and sincerity.
faq.q5: What are the benefits of listening to the Karva Chauth Vrat Katha?
faq.a5: It is believed to bring marital happiness, long life for the husband, family prosperity, and the blessings of Goddess Parvati and Lord Shiva.
faq.q6: Is listening to the Karva Chauth Vrat Katha mandatory?
faq.a6: While not compulsory, listening to or reciting the Katha is considered an essential and highly auspicious part of the Karva Chauth Vrat.
